content:
I just want to second the opinion that pulling for the gacha skin should include other things if we don't immediately pull the skin. I saw someone mention PGR and I think that's the best model to follow. 

- Have a pool with the skin and other cosmetic items, like an avatar, or an avatar frame, or something else. 
- Then, the rest of the pool should be materials, maybe level uncap materials, or skill materials, or exp materials. Probably a mix of all of them would be best.

thread_title: 前瞻：Skin rarity planning 皮肤稀有度规划

content:
Hi Light, I think something that is very important to note is that skin gacha is viewed very negatively by the global audience. Global players are very negative about skin gacha because there are many instances of greedy games using it to squeeze more money. 
I think, even if the skin gacha is good value and gives extra resources, many people will dislike it just because it is called "skin gacha", and many new players may avoid the game because they do not want to play a game with the negative stigma of "skin gacha".

I do not know how it is for chinese players, but at least for global players, it is better to do it as a fixed price. 
The important thing is, at least for me and most global players I know, we want to feel like we got a good deal for our money. The problem with skin gacha is that most of the time you will pay maximum price, so it feels like we are being ripped off, even if the total price is normal. Yes there is a chance to get it at a lower price with good luck, but many times players will not have good luck. This is fine for character pulling because we can farm for pulls, pulling is free if we wait. But skin gacha is paid, so there is no choice but to spend money, so bad luck is much more painful. Plus, skin gacha will not be very often, so it is not like character pulling where bad luck is forgettable because we pull every week and can forget about it by getting lucky on the next character. Skin gachas are rare and will probably only be every few months, so if you have bad luck once, we will be upset about it for a very long time.
thread_title: 前瞻：Skin rarity planning 皮肤稀有度规划

content:
!! Please think of the following situation. You go to the department store to buy a new TV for your house. You already know how much a TV costs, and you have budgeted for that amount. You are ready to spend $500 for the TV because that is the normal price. However, they are doing a special promo! You can roll a lottery, and if you win, you get the TV for half price!

You think, as long as you win the lottery, it will feel good. However, half of the people will not win the lottery. Think about how it will feel to you if you lose, but you see someone else walk up, win the lottery, and get a $250 TV.
Are you happy because you bought the new TV at the price you were expecting? No, you will be upset because you think, "I could have saved 50% on the TV." Now, instead of being happy because you bought a TV at the price you were already prepared to pay, you are mad because you did not get 50% off that you saw someone else get.

You do not think "maybe next time I buy a TV I will win the lottery," because you do not plan to buy a new TV soon. Even if you do, maybe you will lose again and it will feel even more bad. If you feel bad for many months, will you keep buying TVs? Will you keep playing? This is the problem with skin gacha compared to character gacha. Character gacha can feel bad, but it is cheap, short, and easy to keep playing and forget. Skin gacha is expensive, long, and very hard to forget if you get unlucky. Because of this it is very unpopular with global players.
thread_title: 前瞻：Skin rarity planning 皮肤稀有度规划

content:
I think, instead of gacha, it should be a fixed price with benefits. For example, a high-tier time-limited pass. The important thing is, with an event pass, even if the price is similar, we are working towards the skin and "earn" it through our own efforts. It will feel good to "earn" the skin this way, even if the money we spend is the same, because we expect to spend that much money and already know what we are getting for our spending. As long as the price is the same for everyone, I think many global players will be happy because this way, it does not feel like they are being scammed out of their money. Whenever you pay more for something that other people got for cheap, it feels very very bad and you feel scammed.

Also, I think it is very important to give enough rolls with the skins, enough of them that the player feels like they are receiving a good deal. After all, we are spending money anyway, right? We will think about what value we get from spending. For example, if a skin is 3980, a player will think about what they can buy instead with that money. If a light spender is careful about their spending, 3980 can get them over 200 luminous cores. That is what they are comparing with when you offer a skin for 3980 premium currency. They ask, "is this skin worth 4-5 SSR characters?" Most people will probably say no.

However, if you have a pass system like the doresain skin, it will much more value. For example, maybe it comes with 100 character gacha pulls. Compared to the 200+ they could have gotten with that money, it is not actually that much. But to a player, they think "wow, this comes with pulls too, if I were to spend money on those it would cost a lot, so this way I am getting a huge discount on the skin!"
The numbers are examples, maybe you choose to lower it, maybe you choose to raise it. But this is just an example, it is how many normal spenders like me think, we are just happy as long as we believe we get good value out of the money we spend.
thread_title: 前瞻：Skin rarity planning 皮肤稀有度规划

thread_title: 前瞻：Skin rarity planning 皮肤稀有度规划

content:
I think the one star skins would add more interest to the game if they were added at a lower cost instead of 680, since they're basically reused old art with minimal changes.
A lot of players enjoy the game without spending, or by spending minimally. A cheaper skin made with reused art for cheap can add further interest for new and older players, especially given that there might be even more skins in the future. Like me personally, I paid for Doresain's even though it was a slight change from the CGI in the storyline.

Adding a star rating to skins also means that you can release multiple skins of each character, with maybe one simpler one and a limited one at the least each release. This means that the proposed rating is too wide. While the game is gracious with giving out free pulls and currency, if a limited character is released with 4 or 5 star rated skin then it might feel like a predatory practice as these are limited and costly. Meanwhile adding a secondary skin that can be earned through the limited event while also having the limited, expensive skin out, can and will add interest for newer players.
thread_title: 前瞻：Skin rarity planning 皮肤稀有度规划

content:
Adding on that the first and second tier for the proposed rating system feel too similar to one another, with both being slightly reworked old art. This rating system being so wide might also pose a challenge since it's a bit more difficult to assign a rating. For example for Doresain's skin, the model is different from the original, so it should be a ⭐⭐ skin, however the main art is barely different from the CGI it's taken from, so it feels more like a ⭐ skin instead.

here I'll add a reworked rating system that could work instead, as I think it could be beneficial to have the current and soon-to-be skins fit into a three-tiered system while the last one might still take a long while to be used.

⭐: slightly reworked old art/previously used CGI from the story. With minimal changes to the corresponding model in-game. This skin will be distributed via limited-time event rewards or exchanges, and added to the permanent shop after the event.
⭐⭐: brand new key art composition, with potential for outfit changes to the in-game model. VFX and SFX will also be adjusted to match the skin's style. Offered as paid reward in the time-limited even pass. Added to the permanent shop after the event. 
⭐⭐⭐: features dynamic effects, skin-only voice lines, and may have the potential for a unique propagation frenzy story. Features a different in-game model. These will be offered as a time-limited reward and might be available during the characters' future returns', will not be added to the shop.
⭐⭐⭐⭐: represents our highest level of craftsmanship, with boundary-pushing designs. We'll only begin on this rarity tier once we've accumulated enough production experience with skins, which may take about a year. These will appear as limited-time reward and will not return past the event date, nor will be added to the shop. 

At least these are my two cents on the rating system.
thread_title: 前瞻：Skin rarity planning 皮肤稀有度规划

content:
I honestly don't see the point of gacha skin, because skins are a show off and doesn't affect the gameplay.

Those who will dedicate amount of dollars for skin will probbaly spend what ever will be required to get them their skins, and F2P won't care, so you'll just piss off the middle spenders who may potentially not secure a skin with money spent for something that, once again, doesn't affect the gameplay.

Having extra stuffs while gachaing for the skin could lessen the bad feeling, but you might as well just bundle the whole package with skin and stuffs, because if I want a skin, I'll be going to get the skin either way, so it's pointless extra step for me. 

Just give me the skin, the gacha is for the charaters that give opportunity to explore different gameplay and I enjoy planning the ressources and the constraints of doing with what I have.

thread_title: 前瞻：Skin rarity planning 皮肤稀有度规划

content:
Well that is the point, F2P players and players who cannot buy cannot get it at all. The skin gacha is paid only, there is no silver, F2P players cannot roll it at all. That is why I said, this feels bad because the only way to roll it is to spend money, so you must spend money for the chance which is why it feels terrible when you do not get it and have to pay full price.

It is a very different issue if the gacha skin could use normal silver, because then it does not cost money directly, it is accessible to F2P and people can roll it without spending any money. That is a different model entirely and would not have the same problems, in that case a gacha would be fine.
thread_title: 前瞻：Skin rarity planning 皮肤稀有度规划

content: I will say as a player of at least three gachas in which I played well and I will say honestly that I am fed up with the concept of "if you want a skin - pay" or "if you want a skin - spin a gacha for a separate currency" which is impossible to get in the real game. It is not difficult to get silver in our game, there is enough of it, besides, events also give it to us. In principle, in a world where everything goes as we want, it would be good if skins could be bought for silver, that is, the player saved up for a long time avoiding gacha and then spent it on a skin. But since I understand that the developers also need income, they could have made either a gacha with a guarantee that you will get a skin for the same price as if you bought it for real money.
